description: Infrastructure Validation & Test Harness — Verify Docker stack, establish automated integration testing patterns, email-based auth test harness

tasks.title: Docker Compose startup validation
tasks.prompt:
Create a comprehensive Docker infrastructure validation script and test suite.

1. Create `scripts/test-infra.sh` that:
   a. Runs `docker compose -f src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Docker/docker-compose.yml up -d`
   b. Waits for each service to be healthy (with timeout of 120s):
      - DynamoDB Local: `curl -s http://localhost:8042` returns 200/400 (any response means alive)
      - Kong: `curl -s http://localhost:8001/status` returns JSON with `database.reachable`
      - MinIO: `curl -s http://localhost:9000/minio/health/live` returns 200
      - Papercut: `curl -s http://localhost:8025` returns 200 (web UI)
      - Swagger UI: `curl -s http://localhost:8090` returns 200
   c. For each service, prints PASS/FAIL with response details
   d. Exits with code 0 only if ALL services pass

2. Create `scripts/test-dynamo-tables.sh` that:
   a. Runs init-dynamo-local.sh (must be idempotent)
   b. Verifies all 6 tables exist: snapp-users, snapp-networks, snapp-content, snapp-intel, snapp-tx, snapp-notif
   c. For each table, verifies: table status is ACTIVE, correct PK/SK names, GSIs exist with correct names
   d. Uses `aws dynamodb describe-table --endpoint-url http://localhost:8042 --table-name {name}`
   e. Prints table-by-table PASS/FAIL report

3. Create `scripts/test-kong-routes.sh` that:
   a. Queries Kong admin API: `curl http://localhost:8001/services` and `curl http://localhost:8001/routes`
   b. Verifies routes exist for: /api/auth, /api/users, /api/networks, /api/content, /api/intel, /api/tx, /api/notif, /api/linkedin
   c. Verifies JWT plugin is configured on protected routes
   d. Verifies CORS plugin is configured globally
   e. Prints route-by-route PASS/FAIL

4. Create `scripts/test-minio-buckets.sh` that:
   a. Runs init-minio.sh (must be idempotent)
   b. Verifies buckets exist: snapp-media, snapp-client
   c. Uses mc (MinIO client) or curl against MinIO API
   d. Prints bucket-by-bucket PASS/FAIL

5. Create `scripts/validate-all.sh` that runs all 4 test scripts in sequence, aggregates results, and prints a final summary.

All scripts must be executable (chmod +x). All must use color output (green PASS, red FAIL) when terminal supports it. All must be runnable without any manual input.

Also verify that the docker-compose.yml healthchecks actually work — fix any that don't (the DynamoDB Local healthcheck in particular may need adjustment since it checks java version, not the actual service).
tasks.done_when: All 5 scripts exist and are executable. Running validate-all.sh with Docker Compose up produces all-green output. DynamoDB tables verified. Kong routes verified. MinIO buckets verified. Papercut accessible.

tasks.title: Papercut email retrieval helper for automated auth testing
tasks.prompt:
Create a test helper library that can programmatically retrieve emails from Papercut SMTP for automated integration testing of the magic link auth flow.

Papercut SMTP (changemakerstudiosus/papercut-smtp) exposes a web UI but its API varies by version. Investigate what's available on the running container at http://localhost:8025.

1. Create `test/Snapp.TestHelpers/Snapp.TestHelpers.csproj` — a shared class library for test infrastructure.

2. Create `test/Snapp.TestHelpers/PapercutClient.cs`:
   - A typed HTTP client that queries Papercut's API or scrapes its web UI to retrieve emails
   - Method: `Task<List<EmailMessage>> GetMessagesAsync()` — returns all caught emails
   - Method: `Task<List<EmailMessage>> GetMessagesForRecipientAsync(string email)` — filters by recipient
   - Method: `Task DeleteAllMessagesAsync()` — clears the inbox (for test isolation)
   - Method: `Task<string?> ExtractMagicLinkCodeAsync(string email)` — convenience method that:
     a. Gets messages for the email address
     b. Finds the most recent magic link email
     c. Extracts the code from the URL in the email body (regex: `code=([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)`)
     d. Returns the code, or null if not found
   - `EmailMessage` class: Id, From, To, Subject, Body (HTML), ReceivedAt

   If Papercut's API is not sufficient, implement an alternative approach:
   - Option A: Mount a volume for Papercut's mail storage directory and read .eml files directly
   - Option B: Use a different local SMTP container that has a known API (e.g., MailHog has /api/v2/messages, or smtp4dev has /api/Messages). If switching container, update docker-compose.yml.
   - Choose whichever approach works reliably. Document the choice.

3. Create `test/Snapp.TestHelpers/DockerTestFixture.cs`:
   - xUnit IAsyncLifetime fixture that:
     a. Verifies Docker Compose stack is running (checks health endpoints)
     b. If not running, starts it via `docker compose up -d` and waits for health
     c. Clears Papercut inbox at start of each test run
     d. Provides base URLs for all services: DynamoDbUrl, KongUrl, MinioUrl, PapercutUrl
   - This fixture is shared across all integration test projects via `[CollectionDefinition]`

4. Create `test/Snapp.TestHelpers/DynamoDbTestHelper.cs`:
   - Creates and disposes test data in DynamoDB Local
   - Method: `Task<string> CreateTestUserAsync()` — inserts a minimal user, returns userId
   - Method: `Task CleanupAsync(string tableName, string pk, string sk)` — deletes test items
   - Uses the AWS DynamoDB SDK with ServiceURL pointing to localhost:8042

5. Add Snapp.TestHelpers as a project reference in all test .csproj files.

Test the PapercutClient by writing a simple xUnit test that:
- Sends an email to Papercut via SMTP (System.Net.Mail to localhost:1025)
- Retrieves it via PapercutClient
- Asserts the subject and body match

This test must pass with Docker Compose running.
tasks.done_when: PapercutClient can retrieve emails programmatically. DockerTestFixture verifies and bootstraps Docker stack. DynamoDbTestHelper manages test data. Simple email round-trip test passes. All code compiles.

tasks.title: End-to-end auth flow integration test (magic link via Papercut)
tasks.done_when: 8 integration test methods exist. Tests compile. Tests either pass (if auth service is running) or skip with clear message (if not). PapercutClient successfully retrieves test emails. The test structure demonstrates the full auth testing pattern.

tasks.title: Automated API test runner script
tasks.prompt:
Create a comprehensive test runner that validates everything locally.

1. Create `scripts/test-all.sh` that runs in sequence:
   a. Infrastructure validation (scripts/validate-all.sh)
   b. Unit tests: `dotnet test test/Snapp.Shared.Tests/ --logger 'console;verbosity=normal'`
   c. Integration tests: `dotnet test test/Snapp.Service.Auth.Tests/ --logger 'console;verbosity=normal'`
      (and any other integration test projects that exist)
   d. Blazor component tests: `dotnet test test/Snapp.Client.Tests/ --logger 'console;verbosity=normal'` (if exists)
   e. Aggregates results: total tests run, passed, failed, skipped
   f. Generates a test report in `.claude/reports/test-report_{timestamp}.md`
   g. Exit code 0 only if all non-skipped tests pass

2. Create `scripts/start-and-test.sh` — the one-command-does-everything script:
   a. Starts Docker Compose if not running
   b. Waits for all services to be healthy
   c. Runs init scripts (tables, buckets)
   d. Runs test-all.sh
   e. Optionally tears down Docker Compose with --teardown flag
   f. Prints summary

3. Update the README.md or create a CONTRIBUTING.md that documents:
   - Prerequisites (Docker, .NET 9 SDK, AWS CLI)
   - How to start the local environment: `scripts/setup-local.sh`
   - How to run all tests: `scripts/start-and-test.sh`
   - How to run just infrastructure tests: `scripts/validate-all.sh`
   - How to run just unit tests: `dotnet test`
   - How to view caught emails: `http://localhost:8025`
   - How to view API docs: `http://localhost:8090`
   - Port map for all services

All scripts must be executable and runnable without manual input.
tasks.done_when: scripts/start-and-test.sh starts Docker, validates infrastructure, runs all tests, and produces a test report. CONTRIBUTING.md documents the full local dev workflow.

tasks.title: CI-compatible Docker test workflow for GitHub Actions
tasks.prompt:
Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the full test suite on every push/PR.

Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml`:

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '9.0.x'
      - name: Start infrastructure
        run: |
          docker compose -f src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Docker/docker-compose.yml up -d
          # Wait for services...
      - name: Initialize infrastructure
        run: |
          scripts/init-dynamo-local.sh
          scripts/init-minio.sh
      - name: Validate infrastructure
        run: scripts/validate-all.sh
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: dotnet test --logger 'trx' --results-directory TestResults
      - name: Upload test results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: TestResults/
      - name: Tear down infrastructure
        if: always()
        run: docker compose -f src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Docker/docker-compose.yml down -v
```

Make sure:
- Docker Compose services have proper health checks so the wait step works
- AWS CLI is available in the runner (install if needed) for DynamoDB table creation
- The workflow handles the init scripts being in src/Snapp.Infrastructure/Scripts/
- Test results are uploaded as artifacts
- Infrastructure is always torn down, even on failure

Test the workflow file by validating its YAML syntax. Don't actually push — just create the file.
tasks.done_when: .github/workflows/ci.yml exists with valid YAML. Workflow starts Docker, validates infra, runs tests, uploads results, tears down.
